title: Evans_Version
text:
library(httr)

Warning: package 'httr' was built under R version 4.4.1

response <- GET("https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q=Iran&from=2026-05-20&language=en&pageSize=50&apiKey=173ec253cb06408abbf28061323e5fb4")

str(response, max.level = 1)

List of 10
 $ url        : chr "https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q=Iran&from=2026-05-20&language=en&pageSize=50&apiKey=173ec253cb06408abbf28061323e5fb4"
 $ status_code: int 200
 $ headers    :List of 16
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "insensitive" "list"
 $ all_headers:List of 1
 $ cookies    :'data.frame':    0 obs. of  7 variables:
 $ content    : raw [1:37455] 7b 22 73 74 ...
 $ date       : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-06-10 18:51:18"
 $ times      : Named num [1:6] 0 0.00718 0.03334 0.07016 0.12731 ...
  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:6] "redirect" "namelookup" "connect" "pretransfer" ...
 $ request    :List of 7
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "request"
 $ handle     :Class 'curl_handle' <externalptr> 
 - attr(*, "class")= chr "response"


Look at the content.

Warning: package 'jsonlite' was built under R version 4.4.1

library(tibble)

Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.4.1

parsed_response <- fromJSON(rawToChar(response$content))
str(parsed_response, max.level = 1)

List of 3
 $ status      : chr "ok"
 $ totalResults: int 17504
 $ articles    :'data.frame':   50 obs. of  8 variables:


Pull a single article.

trump_article <- parsed_response$articles[4, ]
str(trump_article, max.level = 1)

'data.frame':   1 obs. of  8 variables:

 $ author     : chr NA
 $ title      : chr "Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down"
 $ description: chr "Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions."

 $ content    : chr "It has emerged that the Saudis have attacked Iran, they say in retaliation for Iranian attacks. But significant"| __truncated__


Ehh. Let’s print it out better.

print(trump_article)

4 2026-06-01T15:25:54Z
                                                                                                                                                                                                               content
4 It has emerged that the Saudis have attacked Iran, they say in retaliation for Iranian attacks. But significantly, senior Saudi sources say they made it clear to Tehran that they were acting independ… [+45 chars]



trump_article$content

[1] "It has emerged that the Saudis have attacked Iran, they say in retaliation for Iranian attacks. But significantly, senior Saudi sources say they made it clear to Tehran that they were acting independ… [+45 chars]"






article_finder <- function(subject, 
                           date = format(Sys.Date() - 30, "%Y-%m-%d"), 
                           key = "173ec253cb06408abbf28061323e5fb4") {
  URL <- paste0("https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q=",
                 subject,
                 "&from=",
                 date,
                 "&language=en&pageSize=50&apiKey=",
                 key)
  response <- httr::GET(URL)
  
  parsed_response <- fromJSON(rawToChar(response$content))
}


football_articles <- article_finder("Football")
str(football_articles, max.level = 2)

article_finder <- function(subject, 
                           date = format(Sys.Date() - 30, "%Y-%m-%d"), 
                           key = "173ec253cb06408abbf28061323e5fb4") {
  URL <- paste0("https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q=",
                 subject,
                 "&from=",
                 date,
                 "&language=en&pageSize=50&apiKey=",
                 key)
  response <- httr::GET(URL)
  
  parsed_response <- fromJSON(rawToChar(response$content))
}

title: Evans_Version
text:
Basic EDA
Initial Data Look

str(coaster_data)

spc_tbl_ [635 × 20] (S3: spec_tbl_df/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ Coaster        : chr [1:635] "Adrenaline Peak" "Adventure Express" "Afterburn" "Aftershock" ...
 $ Park           : chr [1:635] "Oaks Amusement Park" "Kings Island" "Carowinds" "Silverwood Theme Park" ...
 $ City           : chr [1:635] "Portland" "Mason" "Charlotte" "Athol" ...
 $ State          : chr [1:635] "Oregon" "Ohio" "North Carolina" "Idaho" ...
 $ Census Region  : chr [1:635] "West" "Midwest" "South" "West" ...
 $ Year Opened    : num [1:635] 2018 1991 1999 2008 2010 ...
 $ AgeGroup       : chr [1:635] "3:Newest" "2:Recent" "2:Recent" "3:Newest" ...
 $ TopSpeed       : num [1:635] 45 35 62 65.6 22 67 35 66 48 50 ...
 $ MaxHeight      : num [1:635] 72 63 113 192 25 ...
 $ Scale          : chr [1:635] "Extreme" "Thrill" "Extreme" "Extreme" ...
 $ Type           : chr [1:635] "Steel" "Steel" "Steel" "Steel" ...
 $ Design         : chr [1:635] "Sit Down" "Sit Down" "Inverted" "Inverted" ...
 $ Drop           : num [1:635] 70 47 110 177 22.5 170 20 147 80 144 ...
 $ Length         : num [1:635] 1050 2963 2956 1204 628 ...
 $ Duration       : num [1:635] 66 140 167 92 47 190 100 143 110 110 ...
 $ Inversions?    : chr [1:635] "Yes" "No" "Yes" "Yes" ...
 $ # of Inversions: num [1:635] 3 0 6 3 0 6 0 0 0 4 ...
 $ Make           : chr [1:635] "Gerstlauer Amusement Rides GmbH" "Arrow Dynamics" "Bolliger & Mabillard" "Vekoma" ...
 $ Latitude       : num [1:635] 45.5 39.4 35.3 47.9 28 ...
 $ Longitude      : num [1:635] -122.7 -84.3 -80.8 -116.7 -82.6 ...
 - attr(*, "spec")=
  .. cols(
  ..   Coaster = col_character(),
  ..   Park = col_character(),
  ..   City = col_character(),
  ..   State = col_character(),
  ..   `Census Region` = col_character(),
  ..   `Year Opened` = col_double(),
  ..   AgeGroup = col_character(),
  ..   TopSpeed = col_double(),
  ..   MaxHeight = col_double(),
  ..   Scale = col_character(),
  ..   Type = col_character(),
  ..   Design = col_character(),
  ..   Drop = col_double(),
  ..   Length = col_double(),
  ..   Duration = col_double(),
  ..   `Inversions?` = col_character(),
  ..   `# of Inversions` = col_double(),
  ..   Make = col_character(),
  ..   Latitude = col_double(),
  ..   Longitude = col_double()
  .. )
 - attr(*, "problems")=<externalptr> 


It does not look like any of the variables (num or chr) need to be switched. We will want to switch some factors.

coaster_data

# A tibble: 635 × 20
   Coaster     Park  City  State `Census Region` `Year Opened` AgeGroup TopSpeed

10 Anaconda    King… Dosw… Virg… South                    1991 2:Recent     50  
# ℹ 625 more rows
# ℹ 12 more variables: MaxHeight <dbl>, Scale <chr>, Type <chr>, Design <chr>,
#   Drop <dbl>, Length <dbl>, Duration <dbl>, `Inversions?` <chr>,
#   `# of Inversions` <dbl>, Make <chr>, Latitude <dbl>, Longitude <dbl>


Removing Latitude and Longitude

coaster_data <- coaster_data |>
  select(-"Latitude", -"Longitude")
